items.title: UnitedHealth blasts health care providers’ ‘egregious’ awards under No Surprises Act - Star Tribune
items.reason: This item directly addresses disputes and awards under the No Surprises Act's IDR process, which is a core implementation mechanism relevant to balance-billing protections and their enforcement.
items.summary: UnitedHealth has publicly criticized healthcare providers for receiving what it characterizes as 'egregious' awards through the Independent Dispute Resolution (IDR) process established under the No Surprises Act. The dispute centers on the amounts providers are being awarded in IDR cases, suggesting tension between insurers and providers over how the arbitration mechanism is being applied in practice. This reflects ongoing implementation challenges with the No Surprises Act's balance-billing protections, which directly impacts revenue-cycle operations for both providers and payers managing out-of-network claim disputes.

items.title: Cigna Must Defend Some Claims in NJ Hospitals’ $100 Million Suit - Bloomberg Law News
items.reason: This item reports a litigation ruling against a major payer (Cigna) in a provider lawsuit involving alleged claims-denial or payment practices, which directly falls within the scope of payer litigation and enforcement.
items.summary: A court ruled that Cigna must defend against some claims brought by New Jersey hospitals in a $100 million lawsuit, according to Bloomberg Law News. The ruling allows the hospitals' suit to proceed on certain counts rather than being dismissed entirely. While the excerpt does not specify the underlying allegations, this represents an enforcement action against a major national payer by hospital providers. Revenue-cycle teams should monitor the outcome as it may address claims processing, payment denial practices, or reimbursement disputes that affect hospital payment collection and payer negotiations.
